title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the API server pod specification file has permissions of 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The API server pod specification file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the API server. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the master node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml Verify that the permissions are 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the master node. For example, chmod 600 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
input_row.default_value: By default, the kube-apiserver.yaml file has permissions of 640 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions checking file permissions on the API server pod specification file, which is a property that can be verified through the Kubernetes API or using kubectl commands.
- The control is related to the configuration of the kube-apiserver, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The audit provides a command that can be executed on the master node, indicating that it can be automated through Kubernetes API calls.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_pod_spec_file_permissions

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the permissions of the kube-apiserver pod specification file located at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml`.
- The specific command to check the permissions would be:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pod kube-apiserver -n kube-system -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].volumeMounts[?(@.mountPath=="/etc/kubernetes/manifests")].name}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the volume mount for the kube-apiserver, which should point to the directory containing the pod specification file.
- The expected permissions for the file should be `600` or more restrictive.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: The permissions of `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` are `600` or more restrictive.
  - Fail: The permissions are less than `600`.
